seoTitle: How to Integrate ClickUp with Notion, Step by Step
metaDescription: Learn how to connect ClickUp and Notion so you can preview live task cards inside Notion pages and sync database fields between both tools.
intro: If your team keeps documentation in Notion but tracks work in ClickUp, connecting the two saves you from constantly copying information between them. This guide covers authorizing the ClickUp connection inside Notion's settings, embedding live task previews on a page, and building a linked database that syncs fields like due dates and assignees between the two tools.

steps.heading: Open Notion's connections settings
steps.body: Go to Settings & Members inside Notion and click the My Connections tab to see available integrations.

steps.heading: Discover and authorize ClickUp
steps.body: Click 'Discover New Connections', find the official ClickUp integration, and pass the OAuth consent screen to select which workspaces to authorize.

steps.heading: Paste a ClickUp link into Notion
steps.body: Drop a ClickUp task URL onto a Notion page and choose 'Paste as Preview' to display a live card showing status, assignee, and due date.

steps.heading: Create a linked database
steps.body: Build a new Notion database and choose the linked ClickUp source option so it can sync with a specific ClickUp Space or List.

steps.heading: Map fields between the two tools
steps.body: Align your Notion database columns with matching ClickUp custom fields, tags, and priority markers.

steps.heading: Test the sync
steps.body: Make a change in ClickUp and confirm it mirrors correctly inside your linked Notion database.

faq.question: Do changes in Notion update ClickUp automatically?
faq.answer: Depending on how the linked database is configured, updates can sync in both directions, though the exact behavior varies by connection setup.

faq.question: Can I preview a ClickUp task without creating a linked database?
faq.answer: Yes, pasting a ClickUp URL onto a Notion page and choosing preview mode gives you a live card without needing a full database sync.

faq.question: Is this integration free to use?
faq.answer: The core connection is free to set up, though your usage may be affected by your existing Notion and ClickUp plan limits.

faq.question: Why isn't my ClickUp task showing live status updates in Notion?
faq.answer: Check that you pasted the link as a preview or mention rather than a plain URL, and confirm the connection still has valid authorization.
